steps.title: Use the decay shortcut
steps.content: In $y=a(b)^x$, the value of $b$ is the multiplier for each $1$-unit increase in $x$. Since the model decreases as $x$ increases, $b$ must be between $0$ and $1$. That already eliminates $1.83$, $18.36$, and $126.35$.

steps.title: Confirm with exponential regression
steps.content: To verify the estimate, enter two readable points on the fitted curve, such as $(1,110)$ and $(9,25)$, then run an exponential regression.

steps.title: Match the multiplier
steps.content: The regression gives $b\approx 0.83$. That is the only plausible decay multiplier among the choices, so choice A is correct.
